purpose: Deepen the Lesson 3.2 callback. Show all three graph scales sequentially with matching equations that accumulate on screen.
visual:
(Sequential Display) Picture Graphs appear one at a time. Equations accumulate below — each stays visible as the next graph appears:
| Step | Graph | Equation | Guide |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| 1 | Scale of 2 (3 symbols) | `3 × 2 = 6` | "Remember reading graphs? Here's a scale of 2. 3 symbols, 3 groups of 2, that's 3 times 2 equals..." |
| 2 | Scale of 5 (3 symbols) | `3 × 5 = 15` (added below first) | "Same graph shape, but scale of 5. Same 3 symbols, but now 3 groups of 5, that's..." |
| 3 | Scale of 10 (3 symbols) | `3 × 10 = 30` (added below previous) | "And scale of 10. 3 groups of 10 is..." |
guide: "Look what happened. Same graph. Same number of symbols. But when we changed the scale, the multiplication changed — the product got bigger. That's because we were multiplying by bigger numbers."
design_note: Visual accumulation of equations makes the pattern growth visible. All three scale families from Unit 1 appear here in rapid succession. Students SEE the unit coherence: M1-M6's graph scales were always multiplication waiting to be computed. Bridges past learning to present understanding.
